title: Consumer and Selector
description: Efficiently rebuilding parts of the UI.

slides.title: Consumer and Selector 🎯
slides.content:
# Optimizing Rebuilds

* `Consumer` – rebuilds its builder whenever the listened object changes.
* `Selector` – rebuilds only when a selected part of the state changes, preventing unnecessary rebuilds.

Use `Selector` to filter which changes trigger a rebuild.

quizData.question: When would you use Selector instead of Consumer?

options.text: When you need to rebuild the entire widget tree
When you want to rebuild only when a specific property changes

quizData.explanation: Selector allows you to listen to only part of the state, avoiding unnecessary rebuilds.

slides.title: Using Selector
slides.content: Suppose a model has two fields, but we only care about one:

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## Use `child` parameter in Consumer/Selector

Pass a child widget that doesn't depend on the state to avoid rebuilding it unnecessarily.
